title: X-Only Public Keys
description: X-only public keys encode only the x coordinate of a secp256k1 point and restrict valid keys to one y-coordinate parity choice. Taproot and Schnorr use this 32-byte form to simplify key handling and reduce witness size.

resources.title: Programming Bitcoin
resources.notes: Deeper walk-through of the cryptography, scripting, and transaction mechanics behind this topic.

resources.title: BIP 340: Schnorr Signatures for secp256k1
resources.notes: Defines the x-only key format used by Bitcoin Schnorr signatures.

resources.title: BIP 341: Taproot
resources.notes: Shows how x-only keys appear in Taproot internal keys and output keys.
